# Chicago Education Digest
_Jul 06 – Jul 20, 2026_

> Covering: K-12 Education & Schools · Board of Education & Governance

## K-12 Education & Schools

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) is currently addressing a projected $732.5 million budget deficit. To close this gap, the district has proposed a budget that includes significant cost-cutting measures. These involve laying off 162 central and citywide employees, with an additional plan to lay off 760 teachers and aides. Other proposals include implementing five furlough days for staff and a spending freeze. Public hearings are scheduled to discuss the budget proposal, which has also reignited discussions about the future of under-enrolled schools within the district.

Alongside these budget challenges, CPS is navigating a substantial increase in special education needs and associated costs, requiring additional staff and separate classroom settings. Funding discussions are ongoing, with CPS leaders calling for more state funding and Cook County exploring options for a late property tax loan to the district. Separately, the district reported that over 700 CPS employees were disciplined for misconduct over the past year, with 10 facing allegations of sexual abuse. In governance news, the Chicago Board of Education recently committed to prohibiting candidates from accepting campaign donations from CPS vendors, while the Chicago Teachers Union endorsed 15 candidates for the school board. School board members have reportedly shown a split on the proposed budget.

## Board of Education & Governance

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) is addressing a significant $732 million budget deficit, leading to a proposed budget that includes substantial cuts. The district plans to lay off 760 teachers and aides, implement five furlough days for staff, and eliminate five student non-attendance days. Public hearings are scheduled to discuss this budget proposal, which has also reignited debate over the continued operation of under-enrolled schools. In response to the financial challenges, Cook County is exploring options to make CPS eligible for a late property tax loan, while at least one school board member has attributed the district's financial woes to delayed county property tax bills rather than state funding. The Chicago Teachers Union has demanded action following the initial layoffs of 162 employees.

In related governance developments, the Chicago Board of Education has committed to and barred candidates from accepting campaign donations from CPS vendors, a move aimed at enhancing transparency. As the district prepares for upcoming school board elections, five candidates, including two incumbents, have been removed from the ballot. The Chicago Teachers Union has endorsed 15 candidates for the school board. Internally, current school board members are reportedly split on the proposed budget that includes the teacher cuts and furloughs.

### Articles

- [Chicago Public Schools to hold public hearings on budget proposal involving hundreds of layoffs - CBS News](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMingFBVV95cUxQMTB5eUZEOTUxREhhdmV5cnZ1YlB1ckdlTmNOS2MwTFZWb0FrTUk4TDg1VEYzVVByU2hHTHIxemtOOXNPdUZJMkZBSmhzMGpZUlV0Q0hVMVppdWRCX2VIb0dkZDdLMWF5anloNGRPUnRsVDFBR2NzNVVub09nSXF6QmhlNi1iZDBocVJwMDY0azJQcS1oZ1A0dGpQTE9kQQ?oc=5) — Google News - Chicago Education, Jul 20, 2026
